description: Tier-3 warm-pool latency + throughput + scaling. Persistent NRP worker pods (queue-group subscribers) over the leaf bridge (real NRP<->Atlas WAN); driver on the Atlas hub. Re-measured 2026-07 over the same continuously-running deployment (leaf up 21d, controller 10d).

setup.workers: ignored-range CPU Job pods (cpu=1, mem=2Gi), queue-group subscribers on subject bench.work, bounded lifetime, auto-cleaned
setup.policy: <=4 pods, ignored range, exit-0, ttl + delete cleanup

finding: Warm-pool per-task latency ~76 ms (WAN-RTT bound) vs ~7.3 s ephemeral-Job cold-start -- the pool removes the ~100x cold-start penalty. Throughput is flat across 1-4 workers (~700-740/s) because for trivial I/O-bound tasks the ceiling is driver_concurrency / RTT (Little's law: 64 / 0.076 ~ 840/s), not worker count; one pod already saturates the link. Worker-count scaling matters for compute-bound tasks. Re-measured over the live production deployment (dogfooded), superseding the earlier ~67 ms / ~930/s run on a faster path.
